commands.helpText:
Available commands:

  /help           Show help
  /clear          Clear the current session
  /model <name>   Switch the Claude model
  /status         Show the current session status

Send plain text to chat with Claude Code.
commands.clearSuccess: ✅ Session cleared. The next message will start a new session.

daemon.permissionTimeout: ⏰ Permission request timed out and was denied automatically.
daemon.processingClear: ⏳ The previous message is still being processed. Clear the session later.
daemon.processingGeneral: ⏳ The previous message is still being processed. Please wait...
daemon.permissionAwaitingReply: Waiting for permission approval. Reply with y or n.
daemon.permissionRequestHint: Reply y to allow, n to deny
daemon.unsupportedMessage: This message type is not supported yet. Please send text or an image.

cli.qrOpened: Opened the QR code image. Please scan it with WeChat:
cli.daemonCommand: Run npm run daemon -- start to launch the service
cli.noAccount: No account found. Run node dist/main.js setup first.
cli.sessionExpired: ⚠️ The WeChat session expired. Run setup again and scan the QR code.

qr.scanPrompt: Scan the QR code above with WeChat

cwd.locked: Locked to the working directory selected in the UI. The daemon will override the session cwd on startup.
cwd.mismatch: The current Claude cwd differs from the UI directory. It will be overridden on the next startup.

error.noPendingPermission: There is no pending permission request
error.sessionExpired: The WeChat session expired. Please sign in again by scanning the QR code.

system.directoryConfirmed: Working directory confirmed. Starting the bridge: {path}
system.modelUpdated: Claude model switched to {model}. The next request will start a new session with the new model.

notification.sessionExpiredBody: Sign in again to keep using the bridge

tray.relogin: Sign In Again (switch account or renew session)

brand.subtitle: Turn your local Claude Code runtime into a desktop bridge that can be reached from WeChat.

hero.title: Scan, connect, and stay resident in the background.
hero.description: This is no longer a script shell hiding in the tray. It now behaves like a real desktop console, with connection state, runtime health, and recent logs visible at a glance.

status.connected: The desktop bridge is connected and running in the background
status.scanned: Scan succeeded. Pick a working directory and click Start Using

qr.title: Scan to Bind
qr.hint: For the first run, scan directly with WeChat. Expired QR codes refresh automatically.

setup.hint: Once started, the bridge daemon keeps running in the background. Closing the window only hides it and does not exit the app.

dashboard.title: Runtime Console
dashboard.subtitle: Inspect daemon health, current session state, recent messages and replies, and the persisted logs.
dashboard.resumeReady: The current Claude session can be reused
dashboard.resumeReset: The next request will start a new session
dashboard.pendingPermissionEmpty: There is no pending permission request

stats.configuredModelHint: Desktop changes are written back to both the config and the current session by default

feed.title: Live Message Feed
feed.subtitle: Messages and logs stay side by side on one screen, so long conversations can scroll here without forcing you to jump to the bottom.
feed.empty: No live messages yet. Once a WeChat message arrives, it will appear here.

logs.title: Recent Logs
logs.subtitle: The latest 120 bridge log lines stay next to the message feed so debugging does not require bouncing around.

details.title: Bridge Details
details.subtitle: Current account info, the WeChat bot endpoint, and the latest message and session state.

controls.title: Claude Controls
controls.subtitle: Current model, the switch entry point, permission mode, and pending tool approvals are all managed here.
controls.modelPlaceholder: default or a custom model name
controls.modelHint: Saving updates both the current session and the global config, clears the old session id, and starts the next request with a new session.
controls.permissionHint: `bypassPermissions` skips tool approval entirely. The bridge also enables the SDK's `allowDangerouslySkipPermissions`. Keep this default only if you knowingly accept that risk.

actions.permissionModeUpdateFailed: Failed to update the permission mode: {error}
actions.preloadMissing: Renderer initialization failed: preload did not load
